---
title: "Advanced Features"
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Advanced Features}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
knitr:
  opts_chunk:
    collapse: true
    comment: "#>"
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(shinyelectron)
```

Four features sit on top of the basic export pipeline: splash screens, the
system tray, native menus, and the in-window preloader. Each solves a
different stretch of the user's first few seconds with your app. This
guide walks through them in order.

## Splash screens

A splash screen fills the window while the backend starts. It replaces a
blank rectangle with something informative.

Reach for a splash screen when:

- **Shinylive is loading.** WebAssembly initialization takes five to ten
  seconds and you need to show the user the app is alive.
- **You want branding.** Logo, product name, a version string.
- **Startup is not instant.** A splash proves the app is starting, not
  frozen.

### Configuration

Add a `splash` block to `_shinyelectron.yml`.

```yaml
splash:
  enabled: true
  duration: 3000          # Minimum display time (ms)
  background: "#ffffff"   # Background color
  image: "splash.png"     # Path relative to app directory
  width: 400
  height: 300
  text: "Loading..."
  text_color: "#333333"
```

Every key, with defaults:

| Option | Description | Default |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `enabled` | Enable splash screen | `false` |
| `duration` | Minimum display time in milliseconds | `3000` |
| `background` | Background color (hex or CSS color) | `#ffffff` |
| `image` | Path to splash image | `null` |
| `width` | Splash window width in pixels | `400` |
| `height` | Splash window height in pixels | `300` |
| `text` | Loading text below image | `Loading...` |
| `text_color` | Text and spinner color | `#333333` |

### Custom splash image

1. Prepare a PNG. 300x200 pixels minimum. Larger is fine.
2. Drop it in your app directory.
3. Point the config at it.

```yaml
splash:
  enabled: true
  image: "assets/splash-logo.png"
  background: "#1a1a2e"
  text: "Starting Dashboard..."
  text_color: "#eaeaea"
```

::: {.callout-tip}
A transparent PNG on a solid background color renders most predictably
across displays.
:::

## System tray

The system tray (menubar on macOS) lets your app live between windows. An
icon sits in the tray; the app runs whether or not a window is open.

Typical uses:

- **Background services** that should keep running when the window is
  closed.
- **Quick reopen** with a tray-icon click.
- **Status indicators** surfaced through the tray menu.

### Configuration

```yaml
tray:
  enabled: true
  minimize_to_tray: true    # Minimize hides to tray
  close_to_tray: false      # Close button hides (not quits)
  tooltip: "My Dashboard"   # Hover text
  icon: "tray-icon.png"     # Custom tray icon (optional)
```

| Option | Description | Default |
|--------|-------------|---------|
| `enabled` | Enable system tray | `false` |
| `minimize_to_tray` | Minimize button hides to tray | `true` |
| `close_to_tray` | Close button hides instead of quitting | `false` |
| `tooltip` | Text shown on hover | App name |
| `icon` | Custom tray icon path | App icon |

### Button behavior

The two toggles combine like this:

| Setting | Minimize button | Close button |
|---------|-----------------|--------------|
| `minimize_to_tray: true, close_to_tray: false` | Hides to tray | Quits app |
| `minimize_to_tray: true, close_to_tray: true` | Hides to tray | Hides to tray |
| `minimize_to_tray: false, close_to_tray: true` | Minimizes normally | Hides to tray |

### Default tray menu

The built-in menu has two entries:

- **Show** brings the window forward.
- **Quit** exits the app.

### Tray icon specs

Each platform expects a different format.

| Platform | Format | Size |
|----------|--------|------|
| macOS | PNG (template) | 16x16 or 32x32 |
| Windows | ICO or PNG | 16x16 to 256x256 |
| Linux | PNG | 16x16 to 512x512 |

::: {.callout-note}
On macOS, supply a template image: solid black with transparency. The
system inverts it automatically for light and dark mode.
:::

## Native application menus

Native menus give users keyboard shortcuts and the menu shapes their
operating system expects.

### Configuration

```yaml
menu:
  enabled: true
  template: "default"       # "default", "minimal", or "custom"
  show_dev_tools: false     # Include DevTools in View menu
  help_url: "https://docs.example.com"
```

### Templates

**Default** ships a full application menu.

- File (Quit, Close)
- Edit (Undo, Redo, Cut, Copy, Paste, Select All)
- View (Reload, Zoom, Fullscreen)
- Window (Minimize, Zoom)
- Help (Documentation, About)

**Minimal** keeps only what most apps actually need.

- File (Quit)
- Edit (Copy, Paste)
- Help (About)

### Platform differences

macOS, Windows, and Linux disagree about where "Quit" lives. shinyelectron
follows each platform's convention.

| Platform | App menu | Keyboard shortcuts |
|----------|----------|--------------------|
| macOS | App-name menu with About, Services, Hide | Cmd+Q, Cmd+H |
| Windows | No app menu, Quit lives under File | Alt+F4 |
| Linux | Same as Windows | Same as Windows |

### DevTools access

Enable DevTools in the View menu when building for development.

```yaml
menu:
  show_dev_tools: true
```

Users reach DevTools via View > Toggle Developer Tools, or with Cmd+Option+I
on macOS and Ctrl+Shift+I on Windows and Linux.

## Preloader

The preloader is a loading indicator shown inside the app window while the
Shinylive WebAssembly runtime boots.

### Configuration

```yaml
preloader:
  enabled: true
  style: "spinner"          # "spinner", "bar", or "dots"
  message: "Loading application..."
  background: "#f8f9fa"
```

### Styles

| Style | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `spinner` | Animated circular spinner |
| `bar` | Horizontal progress bar |
| `dots` | Animated bouncing dots |

### Preloader vs splash screen

They look similar but solve different problems.

| Feature | Splash screen | Preloader |
|---------|---------------|-----------|
| When shown | Before the app window | Inside the app window |
| Duration | Fixed minimum time | Until app is ready |
| Purpose | Branding, startup feedback | Loading feedback |
| Customization | Full custom HTML | Style only |

::: {.callout-tip}
Use both. A splash screen handles the first two or three seconds of
branding, then the preloader takes over until WebAssembly finishes
loading.
:::

## A complete config

All four features, wired up together.

```yaml
app:
  name: "My Dashboard"
  version: "1.0.0"

window:
  width: 1400
  height: 900

# Show branded splash screen on startup
splash:
  enabled: true
  duration: 2500
  background: "#1e3a5f"
  image: "assets/logo.png"
  text: "Loading Dashboard..."
  text_color: "#ffffff"

# Enable system tray for background operation
tray:
  enabled: true
  minimize_to_tray: true
  close_to_tray: true
  tooltip: "My Dashboard - Running"

# Full native menus with documentation link
menu:
  enabled: true
  template: "default"
  show_dev_tools: false
  help_url: "https://docs.example.com"

# Show loading indicator in app
preloader:
  enabled: true
  style: "spinner"
  message: "Initializing..."
```

## Editing the config from R

The config is YAML, so you can edit it from R when you need to.

```{r}
#| eval: false
# Read current config
config <- read_config("path/to/app")

# Enable splash screen
config$splash$enabled <- TRUE
config$splash$duration <- 3000
config$splash$text <- "Loading Dashboard..."

# Enable system tray
config$tray$enabled <- TRUE
config$tray$close_to_tray <- TRUE

# Write updated config
yaml::write_yaml(config, file.path("path/to/app", "_shinyelectron.yml"))
```

## Next steps

- **[Auto Updates](auto-updates.html)**: Keep your app up to date
- **[Configuration](configuration.html)**: Full configuration reference
- **[Troubleshooting](troubleshooting.html)**: Common issues and solutions
